अष्टांग योग

Ashtanga
Yoga

Ashtanga Primary Series

The Standing Sequence · Ashtanga Primary Series

A dynamic, set sequence of postures linked by breath and movement — practiced in the same order every time, building strength, flexibility, and meditative focus through disciplined repetition.

scroll

The Standing Sequence

Thirteen foundational postures, each linked through vinyasa — breath and movement woven into one unbroken thread. This is the sequence taught for the YTT exam, performed in the canonical Ashtanga order set down in Mysore.

Opening

Warm-Up

✦
Kati Chakrasana
↑ Inhale
Sun Salutations

Surya Namaskar A

5 Rounds
1
Urdhva Vrikshasana
2
Uttanasana
↓ Exhale
3
Ardha Uttanasana
↑ Inhale
4
Chaturanga Dandasana
↓ Exhale
5
Urdhva Mukha Svanasana
↑ Inhale
6
Adho Mukha Svanasana
↓ ExhaleUjjayi × 5 breaths
7
Ardha Uttanasana
↑ Inhale
8
Uttanasana
↓ Exhale
9
Urdhva Vrikshasana → Samasthiti
↑ Inhale, arms up↓ Exhale, Samasthiti
Sun Salutations

Surya Namaskar B

5 Rounds
1
Utkatasana
↑ Inhale
2
Uttanasana
↓ Exhale
3
Ardha Uttanasana
↑ Inhale
4
Chaturanga Dandasana
↓ Exhale
5
Urdhva Mukha Svanasana
↑ Inhale
6
Adho Mukha Svanasana
↓ Exhale
7
Virabhadrasana A — Right
↑ Inhale, right leg forward
8
Chaturanga Dandasana
↓ Exhale
9
Urdhva Mukha Svanasana
↑ Inhale
10
Adho Mukha Svanasana
↓ Exhale
11
Virabhadrasana A — Left
↑ Inhale, left leg forward
12
Chaturanga Dandasana
↓ Exhale
13
Urdhva Mukha Svanasana
↑ Inhale
14
Adho Mukha Svanasana
↓ ExhaleUjjayi × 5 breaths
15
Ardha Uttanasana
↑ Inhale
16
Uttanasana
↓ Exhale
17
Utkatasana → Samasthiti
↑ Inhale, arms up↓ Exhale, Samasthiti
Standing Sequence · Overview

Standing Sequence

A
Padangusthasana
↓ Exhale to foldHold × 3↑ Head up to exit
B
Padahastasana
↓ Exhale deeperHold × 3↑ Inhale to rise
Standing Sequence · 3–4

Triangle Group

Step or jump the right leg back. Legs wide, arms extended wide, facing front.

3
Utthita Trikonasana
↓ Exhale to sideHold × 3↑ Inhale to center
4
Parivritta Trikonasana
↓ Exhale to twistHold × 3↑ Inhale to rise
Standing Sequence · 5–6

Side Angle Group

Jump right leg back again. Legs wide, arms extended, facing the mirror.

5
Utthita Parsvakonasana
↓ Exhale to sideUjjayi × 3↑ Inhale to center
6
Parivritta Parsvakonasana
↓ Full exhale into twistHold × 3↑ Inhale to rise
Standing Sequence · 7–10

Prasarita Padottanasana

Jump right leg back. Wide stance, toes turning inward and heels outward.

7
Prasarita Padottanasana A
↓ Exhale, palms downHold × 3↑ Head up, then rise
8
Prasarita Padottanasana B
↓ Exhale, hands on waistHold × 3↑ Inhale to rise
9
Prasarita Padottanasana C
↓ Exhale, arms overheadHold × 3↑ Inhale to rise
10
Prasarita Padottanasana D
↓ Exhale, hold toesHold × 3↑ Head up, then rise
Standing Sequence · 11

Parsvottanasana

11
Parsvottanasana
↓ Exhale to foldHold × 3↑ Inhale to rise
Standing Sequence · 12–13

Standing Balance

12
Utthita Hasta Padangusthasana
↑ Inhale, leg to front↓ Exhale, head to leg↓ Exhale, leg to side
13
Ardha Baddha Padmottanasana
↑ Inhale, arm up↓ Exhale, fold slowHold × 3↑ Inhale to rise
Standing Sequence · 14–16 · Closing

Warrior Sequence

Enter via the first 6 steps of Surya Namaskar A → jump forward between the palms → land in a squat and rise into Utkatasana.

14
Utkatasana
↑ Inhale on landingHold × 3↓ Exhale, jump back
15
Virabhadrasana A
↑ Inhale, step forwardHold × 3 each side
Right side, then Left — no vinyasa between sides
16
Virabhadrasana B
Hold × 3 each side
Left side first (continuing from Warrior A), then Right
Closing Transition

Into Seated

Vinyasa out of Warrior B → Dandasana
→
Exhale — palms to the front of the mat
→
Chaturanga Dandasana — lower down
→
Urdhva Mukha Svanasana — Inhale, Up Dog
→
Adho Mukha Svanasana — Exhale, Down Dog
→
Jump forward — sit in Dandasana
Dandasana
Staff Pose · End of Standing Sequence
Ashtanga Yoga
